title: Butchers
description: Directly supervise and coordinate the activities of production and operating workers, such as inspectors, precision workers, machine setters and operators, assemblers, fabricators, and plant and system operators. Excludes team or work leaders. Cut, trim, or prepare consumer-sized portions of meat for use or sale in retail establishments. Use hands or hand tools to perform routine cutting and trimming of meat, poultry, and seafood. (Based on 10 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Inspect meat products for defects, bruises or blemishes and remove them along with excess fat, cut and trim meat to prepare for packing, and prepare special cuts of meat ordered by customers. Use knives, cleavers, meat saws, and other equipment to perform these tasks, including separating meats and byproducts into specified containers and sealing them for storage.

Produce hamburger meat and meat trimmings, process primal parts into cuts that are ready for retail use, and grind meat into hamburger and into trimmings used to prepare sausages and other meat products. Cut, trim, skin, sort, and wash viscera of slaughtered animals.

Receive, inspect, and store meat upon delivery to ensure meat quality, and record quantity of meat received and issued to cooks or keep records of meat sales. Estimate requirements and order or requisition meat supplies to maintain inventories.

Activate machines to mix or stir ingredients, and then wrap, weigh, label, and price cuts of meat for sale. Prepare and place meat cuts and products in display counter to appear attractive and catch the shopper's eye.
Clean, trim, slice, and section carcasses for future processing, and cure, smoke, tenderize, and preserve meat. Remove parts, such as skin, feathers, scales, or bones from carcass.
Supervise other butchers or meat cutters, and review work orders, specifications, or instructions to determine procedures and adjust the workflow to ensure production goals are met.

Observe gauges, thermometers, and equipment to ensure proper operation and product quality. Notify supervisors when malfunctions occur, keep records of production data, and perform routine maintenance on equipment.
Shape, lace, and tie roasts, and perform cutting, trimming, boning, and grinding of meats, such as beef, pork, poultry, and fish to prepare them in cooking form.
Conduct employee training in equipment operations or work and safety procedures. Confer with other supervisors to coordinate operations and activities within or between departments.
Notify or signal other workers to operate equipment or when processing is complete, and monitor the flow of materials and products through machinery to ensure proper processing.
Remove cooked materials or products from equipment and weigh meats, tagging containers for weight and contents as necessary.
Remove molds and components after production is complete. Remove defective or substandard materials or products as necessary.
Activate equipment such as grinders, canners, and molding presses to prepare or further process products, and clean equipment using hoses and cleaning solutions.
Inspect materials, products, or equipment to detect defects or malfunctions.
Requisition materials, supplies, equipment parts, or repair services.
Confer with management or subordinates to resolve worker problems, complaints, or grievances.
Interpret specifications, blueprints, job orders, and company policies and procedures for workers.
Maintain operations data, such as time, production, and cost records, and prepare management reports of production results.
Recommend or implement measures to motivate employees and to improve production methods, equipment performance, product quality, or efficiency.
Determine standards, budgets, production goals, and rates based on company policies, equipment and labor availability, and workloads.
Set up and adjust machines and equipment.
Calculate labor and equipment requirements and production specifications using standard formulas.
Enforce safety and sanitation regulations.
Plan and develop new products and production processes.
Evaluate employee performance.
